campus.setting: Small-town campus in Northfield, MN, with two campuses (Carleton + St. Olaf)
campus.culture: Intellectually playful, quirky, and collaborative. The trimester system is fast-paced (3 terms per year). Students are genuinely passionate about learning. 'Comps' (comprehensive exams) are a rite of passage. The 'Arb' (arboretum) is a beloved 880-acre natural space.
campus.distinctiveTraits: The trimester system lets students focus on just 3 classes at a time, deeply. The 'Comps' (senior comprehensive projects) are required for every major. The Off-Campus Studies office sends 70%+ students abroad. The 'Arb' is one of the largest college arboreta in the US.
campus.whatTheyLookFor: Carleton values intellectual playfulness, collaboration, and academic depth. They want students who will thrive in the trimester system and embrace the small-town Minnesota setting. The 'Why Carleton?' should mention the trimesters or the Arb.
campus.applicationTips: The 'Why Carleton?' essay should explain why the trimester system appeals to you (deep focus vs. broad sampling). Show you've looked at the Off-Campus Studies options. The 'unusual' essay prompt is a chance to show quirkiness. The Arb is a real selling point for outdoorsy students.
